concise_definition: A roasting phase where coffee beans audibly crack as internal pressure causes structural rupture.
expanded_explanation: First crack marks a major roast milestone and usually indicates transition into lighter-to-medium roast development. Roast operators use it as a repeatable control point for flavor development, balancing acidity, sweetness, and body.
why_it_matters: Learners can better connect roast process decisions to flavor outcomes and describe roast level with more precision.
examples: Roaster logs first crack at 8:40 and extends development by 1:20 for a balanced filter profile.
Two batches with identical charge temp diverge because one enters first crack 30 seconds earlier.
other_ideas: Show first crack timing as a percentage of total roast time.
Contrast underdeveloped vs. well-developed cups around first crack decisions.
infographic_caption: First crack marks a key roast control point where bean structure ruptures and flavor development decisions accelerate.

citations.title: National Coffee Association - Coffee Roasting Overview
